mnemonics: C A I N: To remember the four group axioms (Closure, Associativity, Identity, iNverse).
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that combines any two elements to form a third, while satisfying four fundamental properties: closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility.
The identity element of a group is unique, and each element has a unique inverse within the group.
Groups can be finite (like the integers modulo n under addition) or infinite (like the set of all integers under addition).
commonMistakes: Assuming all mathematical structures with a binary operation are groups (e.g., natural numbers under addition are not a group because they lack additive inverses).
Forgetting to check the closure property for a subset when determining if it is a subgroup.
